q: Electrical energy can be transformed into a wide variety of other useful forms of ___.
The transformation of electrical energy into ___ energy is very useful and important.
Electrical energy is provided by source of ___ such as batteries and generators.
A steady current will flow through the conductor if a steady ___ is maintained between its ends.
Batteries and generators are called ___ of electromotive force.
A battery converts ___ energy into electrical energy to provide electrical power.
In an electric generator, ___ energy is converted into electrical energy.
A source of electromotive force has a positive terminal and a ___ terminal.
Positive charges are sent from the ___ terminal to the positive terminal within the source.
The main function of the source is to send negative charges from the ___ terminal to the negative terminal within the source.
Electromotive force, abbreviated as ___, is the amount of energy provided by the battery per unit charge to move round the complete circuit.
Emf is measured in unit of ___ ($V$).
One volt is equivalent to ___ of electric charge ($J\ C^{-1}$).
Despite its name, electromotive force is not actually a ___.
Generally, a source of electromotive force has an ___ resistance.
The internal resistance of a battery depends on its size, chemical properties, ___, and temperature.
A resistor of resistance $r$ must be regarded as being connected in ___ to the battery.
The potential difference across the resistor is $V = ___$.
The total work done in moving a unit positive charge round the complete circuit is $IR + Ir$, which is the ___ $E$ of the battery.
The equation $I = \frac{E}{R+r}$ is called the ___ equation.
The potential difference between the terminals of a battery when it is connected to an external circuit is given by $V = ___$.
When a battery having an internal resistance is used in an electric circuit, the potential difference between the terminals of the battery is always ___ than its emf.
The potential difference across the terminals of a battery connected to an external circuit is called the ___ voltage.
Terminal voltage is also referred to as ___ voltage.
Emf is an ___ voltage.
Terminal voltage is a ___ voltage.
___ a battery means the chemical energy of the battery which has been used up is now supplied back by electrical energy from some external source.
In charging a battery, the potential difference between the terminals is equal to $___$.
